# OpenAI SDK Compatibility Decision Matrix

This matrix classifies the metadata-only SDK shape fixtures and the explicit
unsupported `/v1` endpoints probed by SDKs. It is a planning contract for Tasks
4-10, not a claim of broad OpenAI API coverage.

Decisions are limited to `accept`, `translate`, `reject`, or `passthrough`:

- `accept`: codex-pooler accepts the narrow shape as already compatible with its current OpenAI compatibility boundary
- `translate`: codex-pooler accepts the narrow SDK shape and rewrites it into a Codex-compatible upstream request shape
- `reject`: codex-pooler should return a deterministic OpenAI-shaped error before upstream dispatch
- `passthrough`: codex-pooler accepts the narrow shape and forwards the relevant field or item upstream without owning the resulting upstream state

| ID | SDK | Version | Endpoint | Scenario | Observed shape summary | Decision | Owner task | Code target | Test target | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `openai-node.responses.basic_text.v1` | OpenAI Node `openai` | `6.39.0` | `POST /v1/responses` | Basic Responses text creation with one synthetic user message | Top-level `model`, `input`, `stream`; one user message represented as one `input_text` part | `translate` | 6 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs` | Baseline Responses text shape stays narrow; Task 6 owns adjacent Responses field coverage while structured output, reasoning, and service tier cases are expanded |
| `openai-responses.truncation.auto_disabled.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses` | Responses truncation control emitted by SDK clients | Top-level `truncation` is a string; only `auto` and `disabled` are accepted | `accept` | 15 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Matrix` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs` | Accepted for public SDK compatibility but not forwarded upstream; unsupported values reject locally before dispatch |
| `openai-responses.reasoning_context.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses` | Responses reasoning context control emitted by SDK clients | Nested `reasoning.context` is a string; accepted values are `auto`, `current_turn`, and `all_turns` after trim/lowercase normalization | `accept` | reference update 2026-06-17 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | Accepted values are forwarded through the Responses adapter. Unknown, empty, and non-string values reject locally with `reasoning.context` before upstream dispatch |
| `openai-python.chat.basic_tool.v1` | OpenAI Python `openai` | `2.38.0` | `POST /v1/chat/completions` | Basic Chat Completions request with one synthetic user message and one nested function tool | Top-level `model`, `messages`, `tools`, `tool_choice`, `stream`; Chat tool uses nested `function` object with `parameters` | `translate` | 5 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Chat`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/chat_completions_controller_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs` | Official SDK chat shape requires `messages`; chat nested function tools are translated through the Chat compatibility adapter |
| `local-chat.input_fallback.absent_or_empty_messages.v1` | Local compatibility fallback | n/a | `POST /v1/chat/completions`; `POST /backend-api/codex/v1/chat/completions` | Responses-shaped client payload sent to the chat route without usable `messages` | Top-level `input` is accepted only when `messages` is absent or `[]`; non-empty `messages` stays authoritative; omitted fallback `instructions` becomes `""` | `translate` | 5 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Chat`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/chat_completions_controller_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | This is a codex-pooler local fallback only, not broad Responses-to-chat parity and not an official OpenAI SDK chat request shape |
| `vercel-ai.responses.tool_output_continuation.v1` | Vercel `ai` | `6.0.191` | `POST /v1/responses` | Responses tool-output continuation with stored response state flag and previous response reference | Top-level `previous_response_id`, `store`, `input`, `tools`; input item types include `item_reference`, `function_call_output`, and `message:user`; flat function tool shape | `passthrough` | 4 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.Payloads.ToolResultShape`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.Payloads.InputShape` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs` | Current product boundary accepts the observed narrow continuation shape and forwards `previous_response_id`; fake previous ids may fail upstream with an OpenAI-shaped upstream status rather than local state ownership |
| `vercel-ai-sdk-openai.responses.flat_function_tool.v1` | Vercel OpenAI provider `@ai-sdk/openai` | `3.0.65` | `POST /v1/responses` | Provider-specific Responses request with flat function tool definition | Top-level `model`, `input`, `tools`, `tool_choice`; tool keys are flat `type`, `name`, `description`, `parameters` | `accept` | 5 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs` | Responses flat function tools are accepted only for the documented narrow function shape; malformed tool and `tool_choice` variants stay guarded |
| `openai-responses.non_strict_function_schema_lowering.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses`; `POST /backend-api/codex/responses`; `GET /backend-api/codex/responses` websocket `response.create` | Non-strict function tool schema using SDK-permissive JSON Schema variants | Function tool `parameters` may contain boolean schema nodes, `const`, missing object or array type markers, missing object `properties`, missing array `items`, and unsupported descriptive JSON Schema keywords | `translate` | reference update 2026-06-17 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.Payloads.ToolSchemaLowering`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/payloads/payload_normalizer_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | Lowering is limited to non-strict function tools, including nested namespace function tools. Strict function tools and strict structured-output schemas stay strict and are not loosened |
| `vercel-ai-sdk-openai.responses.namespace_function_tool.v1` | Vercel OpenAI provider `@ai-sdk/openai` | `3.0.65+` | `POST /v1/responses` | Provider-specific Responses request with function tools grouped by `providerOptions.openai.namespace` | Top-level `tools` entry has `type=namespace`, nonblank `name`, nonblank `description`, and nested function tools with flat `type`, `name`, `description`, `parameters`, optional `strict`, and optional `defer_loading` | `accept` | namespace tool backport | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.Payloads.StrictSchema` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs` | Accepted only for namespace entries containing nested function tools. Hosted tools, tool_search namespace markers, top-level deferred function tools, empty namespace labels, empty nested tool lists, malformed nested parameters, and non-boolean nested `strict`/`defer_loading` reject locally |
| `openai-responses.additional_tools.request_input_item.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | Node `openai` `6.41.0`; Python `openai` `2.40.0` | `POST /v1/responses`; `POST /v1/chat/completions` fallback input only | Request-shaped Responses `additional_tools` input item | Input item has `type=additional_tools`, `role=developer`, required `tools`, and optional nonblank string `id`; it stays inside `input` | `accept` | 5 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | Accepted only as request-shaped Responses input item. It is non-executable, not top-level, not merged into top-level `tools`, and not sufficient for `tool_choice`; nested remote MCP tool definitions remain rejected. Output-shaped `additional_tools` request input remains rejected |
| `opencode.responses.replay_continuation.v1` | OpenCode `opencode-ai` | `1.15.12` | `POST /v1/responses` and `GET /v1/responses` websocket `response.create` | Responses replay continuation with assistant output, reasoning summary, function call with optional `namespace`, and structured tool output item replay | Top-level `previous_response_id`, `store`, `input`, `tools`; input item types include `message:assistant`, `reasoning`, `function_call`, and `function_call_output`; replayed `function_call` may include `namespace`; content part types include `output_text`, `summary_text`, `input_text`, and `input_image` | `translate` | opencode v1 replay fix | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs` | Accepts only the narrow opencode replay item shapes; unsupported hosted-tool items and image `file_id` references remain rejected locally |
| `hermes.responses.assistant_tool_call_replay.v1` | Hermes Agent | observed 2026-06-08 local state shape | `POST /v1/responses` | Chat-style assistant tool-call replay followed by a role=tool terminal result | Top-level `previous_response_id`, `store`, `input`; assistant item has `role=assistant`, empty `content`, and `tool_calls` with nested `function.name`/`function.arguments`; tool item has `role=tool`, `tool_call_id`, and object `content.output` | `translate` | Hermes replay fix | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | Translates only the observed narrow replay: assistant `tool_calls` become Responses `function_call` input items and role=tool object content uses the string `output` field. Raw terminal output remains metadata-only and unsupported tool-call variants still reject locally |
| `hermes.responses.reasoning_tool_replay.v1` | Hermes Agent | observed 2026-06-08 Hermes 0.16 state shape | `POST /v1/responses` | Encrypted reasoning replay followed by Hermes' empty assistant message, a Responses `function_call`, and a role=tool terminal result | Top-level `previous_response_id`, `store`, `input`; input sequence is `reasoning`, empty `role=assistant` `content`, `function_call`, then `role=tool` with `tool_call_id` and object `content.output` | `translate` | Hermes reasoning replay fix | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs` | Preserves the reasoning replay, converts the empty assistant message to an assistant `message` with `output_text`, preserves the function call, and translates the role=tool object output to `function_call_output`. Encrypted reasoning, terminal output, previous response id, and call id remain metadata-only |
| `hermes.responses.completed_assistant_replay.v1` | Hermes Agent | observed 2026-06-09 Hermes 0.16 state shape | `POST /v1/responses` | Ordinary replay without `previous_response_id` that includes encrypted reasoning, a completed assistant message, and a follow-up user message | Top-level `model`, `store`, `input`, SDK controls; assistant replay item has `type=message`, `role=assistant`, `id`, `phase`, `status=completed`, and `output_text` content | `translate` | Hermes replay fix | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs` | Accepts only safe assistant message status metadata values on replay items. Raw prompt text, encrypted content, and message ids remain metadata-only in tests and fixtures |
| `openclaw.responses.assistant_thinking_replay.v1` | OpenClaw | observed 2026-06-09 OpenClaw 2026.6.1 session state shape | `POST /v1/responses` | Ordinary replay without `previous_response_id` during a second `--session-key` turn where the prior assistant message contributes a reasoning replay item and assistant text replay | Top-level `model`, `store`, `input`; OpenClaw session state contains assistant `thinking` plus `text`, while the transport sends `reasoning` with `content: []` plus assistant `message` with `output_text` annotations | `translate` | OpenClaw replay fix | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/continuation_test.exs` | Drops raw assistant `thinking` replay metadata, removes unsupported reasoning `content: []`, and strips assistant `output_text` annotations before upstream dispatch. Raw prompts, thinking content, signatures, and transport bodies remain metadata-only |
| `vercel-ai-sdk-openai.responses.detailed_reasoning_summary_default.v1` | Vercel OpenAI provider `@ai-sdk/openai` | `4.0.0-beta.75` | `POST /v1/responses` | Provider default reasoning summary when reasoning effort is configured and reasoningSummary is not disabled | Top-level `reasoning` object contains `effort=high` and `summary=detailed` with ordinary text input placeholders | `accept` | reference update 2026-06-20 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs` | `reasoning.summary=detailed` stays in the explicit allowlist and is forwarded as part of the supported Responses reasoning object |
| `codex.responses.web_search.indexed.v1` | Codex native Responses shape | Codex reference `3a2712ea141a5acb243689f803f26e48ff751771` | `POST /v1/responses` | Indexed hosted web search request generated by Codex native tool spec | Tool entry has `type=web_search`, required boolean `external_web_access=true`, and optional boolean `index_gated_web_access=true`; cached search omits the optional flag with `external_web_access=false` | `passthrough` | reference update 2026-06-20 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/runtime/compatibility_contract_test.exs` | Only this Codex-native boolean flag shape is accepted. OpenAI hosted options such as filters, search context size, and user location remain rejected locally |
| `openai-responses.web_search_preview.type_only.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses` | Advanced hosted web search tool with no local options | Tool entry contains only `type=web_search_preview` | `passthrough` | 9 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs` | Exact type-only passthrough remains allowed; configured hosted web search options remain rejected except the Codex-native boolean `web_search` access flags |
| `openai-responses.image_generation.exact_known.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses` | Advanced image_generation tool on Responses compatibility surface | Tool entry is either type-only passthrough or the exact `/v1/images/*` translated keys `type`, `model`, `size`, `quality`, optional `background`, optional `input_fidelity`, optional `output_format` | `passthrough` | 9 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Images` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/images_controller_test.exs` | Task 9 does not broaden direct Responses image tool options beyond exact known shapes; media/image routes keep their existing exact translation contract |
| `openai-responses.advanced_hosted_tools.unsupported.v1` | OpenAI-compatible Responses shape | n/a | `POST /v1/responses` | Unsupported hosted/advanced tool families including top-level `tools[type=mcp]`, nested `input[type=additional_tools].tools[type=mcp]`, file_search, code_interpreter, computer_use, shell, local shell, apply_patch, tool_search, and deferred variants | Tool entries use hosted tool `type` values, including remote MCP locators such as `server_url`, `connector_id`, and recent SDK `tunnel_id`, or deferred markers rather than flat function-tool or accepted namespace-function contracts | `reject` | 9 | `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses`; `CodexPooler.Gateway.OpenAICompatibility.Responses.Input` | `test/codex_pooler/gateway/openai_compatibility/core_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/responses_controller_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/chat_completions_controller_test.exs` | Remote MCP and unknown hosted tool runtime semantics are not implemented. They reject locally before upstream dispatch with deterministic OpenAI-shaped `invalid_request` errors, not `unsupported_endpoint` |
| `unsupported.v1.responses.retrieve.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `GET /v1/responses/:id` | Response retrieval endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Path-only unsupported route; no request body is parsed after auth and compatibility gates | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; codex-pooler does not own public `/v1` response retrieval state |
| `unsupported.v1.responses.cancel.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/responses/:id/cancel` | Response cancellation endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Path-only unsupported route; malformed JSON is not parsed after auth and compatibility gates | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; cancellation is not implemented for public `/v1` response ids |
| `unsupported.v1.responses.delete.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `DELETE /v1/responses/:id` | Response deletion endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Path-only unsupported route; no upstream dispatch or gateway reservation | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; deletion is not implemented for public `/v1` response ids |
| `unsupported.v1.images.variations.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/images/variations` | Image variation endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Unsupported multipart-capable route returns before multipart parsing | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs`; `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/images_controller_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; supported image routes remain limited to generation and edit surfaces |
| `unsupported.v1.embeddings.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/embeddings` | Embeddings endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Unsupported JSON route preserves auth and compatibility gates before oversized body parsing | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; no embedding model compatibility is implied |
| `unsupported.v1.batches.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/batches` | Batch API endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Unsupported JSON route preserves auth and compatibility gates before oversized body parsing | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; batch lifecycle is outside the current product boundary |
| `unsupported.v1.moderations.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/moderations` | Moderations endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Unsupported JSON route returns deterministic OpenAI-shaped error after auth and compatibility gates | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; moderation APIs are not implemented |
| `unsupported.v1.fine_tuning.jobs.v1` | SDK-probed OpenAI public route | n/a | `POST /v1/fine_tuning/jobs` | Fine-tuning job endpoint exposed by SDKs but not implemented by codex-pooler | Unsupported JSON route returns deterministic OpenAI-shaped error after auth and compatibility gates | `reject` | 8 | `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedRoutes`; `CodexPoolerWeb.V1.UnsupportedController` | `test/codex_pooler_web/controllers/v1/route_auth_test.exs` | Expected status `404`; OpenAI-shaped error code `unsupported_endpoint`; fine-tuning lifecycle is outside the current product boundary |
